key_risks: A hot April PCE inflation release on May 28 could lift yields and trigger a growth-stock and semiconductor selloff.
The very strong recent moves in South Korea, Taiwan, and semiconductors could reverse sharply on profit-taking before the June 2 exit.
Any negative company-specific or supply-chain news in major chip constituents could disproportionately hurt the concentrated portfolio.
A broad risk-off move from weak GDP, corporate profits, or ISM manufacturing data could favor defensive sectors over high-beta technology.
Currency weakness in the Korean won or Taiwan dollar versus the US dollar could reduce US-listed ETF returns.

portfolio.rationale: Strongest liquid thematic exposure to the AI/chip cycle, with exceptional 7-day and 30-day momentum and supportive semiconductor sales data.
High beta to global semiconductors and AI hardware, with very strong recent price momentum into the scoring window.
Explosive near-term and medium-term momentum, likely reflecting semiconductor/export strength and risk-on flows.
Broad US technology exposure adds participation in the same leadership trend with somewhat less single-industry concentration than semiconductors.
Captures broad trend persistence across recent market leaders during a short one-week horizon.

portfolio_rationale: The portfolio is deliberately tilted toward the strongest short-horizon leadership: semiconductors, AI-linked technology, Taiwan, South Korea, and momentum. For a one-week relative-return contest, recent trend persistence and concentrated exposure to the dominant market theme appear more attractive than defensive diversification.
rationale_summary: Semiconductor-linked assets have the best trailing momentum and supportive fundamental data, while financial conditions and credit spreads remain benign enough for risk appetite to persist. The main bet is that AI and chip leadership continues through the May 26 to June 2 close-to-close window.
